details:
The npm package `antsrcsrctest` is a supply-chain credential- and environment-stealer disguised as a benign date utility (its `index.js` exports a harmless `formatDate()` function as a decoy). Its real behaviour lives in an install script.

A `preinstall` lifecycle hook (`preinstall: node preinstall.js`) executes automatically on `npm install`, before the package is ever imported. The script fingerprints the host and container (`/.dockerenv`, `/proc/1/cgroup`, `/proc/self/mountinfo`), runs local recon commands (`id`, `whoami`, `hostname`, `ps aux`) via `child_process.execSync`, queries the cloud instance-metadata service at `http://100.100.100.200/latest/meta-data/` (Alibaba Cloud IMDS) — explicitly walking `/latest/meta-data/ram/security-credentials/` to obtain the host's temporary RAM/IAM role credentials — and collects the entire `process.env`. It then exfiltrates the full recon object and the environment via HTTP POST to the hardcoded external IP `http://101.35.44.248` (paths `/` and `/env`). All error paths are swallowed so the install appears normal. The package name targets an internal `antsrc*` namespace, consistent with a dependency-confusion attack against build environments.

Detected and classified independently by codelake Research from the live npm feed on 2026-07-09; at the time of reporting the package was still live on npm and not present in OSV or GHSA (a first-catch).
